rulings.Case Title: F. ALIOTO COMPANY, INC, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adn Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses From Plaintiff F. Alioto Company, Inc. To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 7, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT EXXONMOBIL OIL CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses From Plaintiff F. Alioto Company, Inc. To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Request For Production Of Documents. Defendants motion to compel supplemental responses to special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted in part and denied in part. As to special interrogatories 56-58, 59-61, 62-64 (damages), the motion is granted. Defendant is not seeking the analysis of the damages issue but documents and names of persons with knowledge of the damage. Plaintiff must provide these within 15 days of notice of entry of the order. As to special interrogatories 44-46 (medical bills), the motion is granted. Plaintiff must either provide the requested discovery or confirm in verified responses that it is not seeking damages for medical bills of health issues of its employees. As to R.F.P.O.D. 22 the motion is granted. Plaintiff must provide documents relating to its profits and losses from 1991 to the present. As to R.F.P.O.D. 25 the motion is off calendar as moot. The requested information has been provided. As to R.F.P.O.D. 26 requesting all documents between plaintiff and its financial advisors the motion is denied. This request is impermissibly overbroad. As to R.F.P.O.D. 41, the motion is granted. While the issues of the two cases differ slightly, the damages claim appear similar to the extent plaintiff has already recovered for some of the damages it seeks in this case, it should be precluded. Accordingly, the information sought is relevant. Furthermore, plaintiff's counsel should provide a privilege log on documents it claims are subject to the attorney-client or work product privileges. =(302/LMG)
